div.p: A reusable OWL design system for Odoo 19 built from shadcn/ui patterns, translated into OWL components, services, tokens, and QWeb-friendly APIs.

CardHeader.CardDescription: CSS variables + persisted light, dark, and system modes.
Variants and sizes are exported through a reusable cva-like helper.
Service-driven notifications now map onto exported toast primitives, pause dismissal while users interact, and support swipe dismissal on the toast surface.

CardContent.p: Use useService("odx_toast") from any OWL component. Action buttons, close controls, and swipe interactions keep the timer paused while the toast is being interacted with.

CardHeader.CardDescription: Field wrappers generate ids, descriptions, errors, and required markers with shadcn-style structure.

Card.CardFooter: Slot-scoped control attrs keep labels, descriptions, and validation messages synchronized.

CardHeader.CardDescription: Focus trap, portal rendering, composed overlay and content primitives, and preventable escape or outside-dismiss hooks.

DialogHeader.DialogDescription: Dialog content is rendered through Owl portal APIs and keeps keyboard focus inside the modal.

CardHeader.CardDescription: Legacy item arrays and composed menu primitives share the same shadcn-like surface and keyboard model, accept side or align placement props, and mirror fallback submenu direction in RTL layouts.
Checkbox, switch, separator, and status badges share the same token palette.

div.p: A tight spacing mode for dense backend workspaces and builders.
A switch-style control for reversible workspace preferences.
Indeterminate state is useful for partial selection or inherited permissions before the user resolves the final value.

CardHeader.CardDescription: Dense toolbars, OTP entry, grouped inputs, native fallbacks, and keyboard hints share the same visual system, including direction-aware OTP navigation.

div.p: OTP slots keep arrow-key movement, backspace behavior, paste distribution, a single hidden form value, and RTL-aware horizontal slot navigation.

CardHeader.CardDescription: Disclosure shells and field wrappers provide the layout layer used by denser settings and onboarding flows.

FieldContent.FieldDescription: Native select fallback keeps browser semantics while matching the shared tokens.
Horizontal field rows mirror the compact settings layouts used across shadcn examples.
The field layer can show aggregated validation messages without coupling itself to a specific form library.

CardHeader.CardDescription: Reusable item scaffolds standardize media, titles, descriptions, and action rows for list-heavy modules.
Overlay families now expose dedicated trigger, overlay, and content primitives where appropriate, plus shadcn-style side and align placement props on the lightweight surfaces.

div.p: Popover content can host the same form controls as any full page view.

TooltipContent.text: 'Tooltip surfaces mirror the shadcn compact helper style, open immediately on focus, and can inherit their delay from a shared provider.'
'Move across nearby triggers and the shared provider skips the second hover delay for faster scanning.'

AlertDialogHeader.AlertDialogDescription: Archived workspaces remain restorable from the activity log.

div.p: Alert dialogs reuse the core dialog primitive but lock the footer into a predictable cancel and confirm pattern.

CardHeader.CardDescription: Reusable empty shells give apps and portal builders a consistent fallback composition.

EmptyHeader.EmptyDescription: Use InputGroup, Kbd, and ButtonGroup together to build richer search and empty-state flows without one-off styling.

CardHeader.CardDescription: Structured disclosure now supports shadcn-style headers, disabled items, and trigger keyboard navigation without changing the OWL-native API, including RTL-aware horizontal movement.

AccordionContent.p: Light, dark, and semantic surface tokens are defined once and reused across backend and portal bundles.
Components follow slot-based OWL composition instead of React wrappers, so other modules can extend them naturally.
The same asset bundle can power admin pages, portal previews, builders, and module-level client actions.

CardHeader.CardDescription: Identity and loading states are lightweight primitives designed for repeated reuse, with avatars now exposing dedicated image and fallback parts.
Alert, progress, label, and radio group cover status-heavy workflows.

Alert.AlertDescription: The current batch focuses on reusable primitives that other Odoo modules can compose without pulling Tailwind or React wrappers.

div.p: A progress primitive for onboarding, sync, or long-running background actions.
Indeterminate mode works when the task has started but the backend cannot yet expose precise completion.

div.Label: Manual review
div.p: Editors approve every change before rollout.

div.Label: Staged deploy
div.p: Changes land in a preview environment before they go live.

div.Label: Automatic
div.p: Trusted builders can push straight through the workflow.

CardHeader.CardDescription: Analytics primitives provide reusable legends, tooltips, and token-driven series colors for backend and portal reporting surfaces.

div.p: A shared line chart for signups and visitor volume, with tooltip and legend content driven by the same chart config object.
Grouped bars compare work shipped across portal, builder, and backend tracks.
A donut view summarizes how design-system work is distributed across product surfaces, with the same composed legend and tooltip parts reused across chart types.

CardHeader.CardDescription: Side-docked overlays now expose composed overlay, content, and close primitives while reusing the dialog behavior model, with logical start or end anchoring.

SheetHeader.SheetDescription: Sheets are useful for secondary editing flows that should stay close to the current page context.

CardHeader.CardDescription: Bottom and edge-aligned drawers now expose composed overlay, content, and close primitives on top of the shared labelled-dialog semantics, with logical start or end support for horizontal docking.

DrawerHeader.DrawerDescription: Drawers reuse dialog focus trapping while shifting the visual rhythm toward stacked mobile and portal workflows.

div.p: Shared tokens keep portal approval surfaces aligned with the admin client.
Focus stays inside the drawer until it closes, then returns to the original trigger.
Footer actions can dispatch the same shared services as any other OWL component.

CardHeader.CardDescription: Split layouts cover inspectors, editors, and preview canvases while preserving keyboard-operable panel resizing, including RTL-aware horizontal separator keys.

div.p: Dense navigation for tokens, forms, overlays, and docs sections.
The center workspace can flex wider without forcing a separate one-off layout primitive.
Handles respond to arrow keys as well as pointer dragging for accessible split views.

CardContent.p: Focus a handle and use arrow keys to resize adjacent panels in 5% steps.

CardHeader.CardDescription: Choice surfaces now cover plain pickers, searchable command-style selection, buffered typeahead, stable listbox linkage, and the same side or align placement API as the other overlay roots.

div.p: A non-searchable select for small, known option sets. With focus on the trigger, typing will jump to the next matching option.
The same select root can now be authored with composed OWL primitives instead of only an items array.

SelectItem.description: 'Release in controlled slices before full exposure.'

SelectItemDescription.text: 'Release in controlled slices before full exposure.'

SelectItem.description: 'Route a narrow percentage of traffic before the broader rollout.'

SelectItemDescription.text: 'Route a narrow percentage of traffic before the broader rollout.'

SelectItem.description: 'Delay activation until the next approved release window.'

SelectItemDescription.text: 'Delay activation until the next approved release window.'

SelectItem.description: 'Hold the launch for the overnight stability window.'

SelectItemDescription.text: 'Hold the launch for the overnight stability window.'

SelectItem.description: 'Apply the rollout without a manual checkpoint.'

SelectItemDescription.text: 'Apply the rollout without a manual checkpoint.'

div.p: A searchable combobox for larger collections that now exposes composed trigger, value, icon, and content primitives on top of the command surface.
The same root can be authored with explicit trigger and content wrappers when a workflow needs custom command composition.

CardHeader.CardDescription: Searchable command surfaces support both config-driven data and composed input, list, group, and item primitives.

div.p: Pass a normalized item array when the command surface is backed by registry or route metadata.
Build custom palettes with explicit groups, separators, empty states, and shortcut content.

CommandItem.label: 'Toggle theme'
CommandItem.description: 'Switch between the current light and dark palettes.'

CommandItemDescription.text: 'Switch between the current light and dark palettes.'

CommandItem.label: 'Show success toast'
CommandItem.description: 'Dispatch a shared toast event from the active service.'

CommandItem.label: 'Open portal preview'
CommandItem.description: 'Jump to the public preview route for the same asset bundle.'

CommandDialog.title: 'Command Palette'
CommandDialog.description: 'Search for a command to run across the current workspace.'

CardHeader.CardDescription: Navigation and data-display primitives are ready for admin surfaces, builders, and portals, with direction-aware breadcrumb and pagination affordances plus logical start or end table alignment.
Client-side search, sorting, selection, pagination, row actions, and root direction hooks turn the base table styles into an app-ready admin surface.
Compact action toggles and hover-driven identity panels cover dense toolbars and rich previews.

div.p: Single toggles and grouped toggles use the same pressed-state treatment.
Hover cards are useful for author previews, maintainer identity, and lightweight relationship context without fully switching views.

CardHeader.CardDescription: Layout wrappers and continuous controls now cover both single-value adjustment and minimum-spaced range selection.

t.t-esc: `${form.releaseGuardrail[0]}%-${form.releaseGuardrail[1]}% rollout band with enforced spacing between thumbs.`

div.p: Aspect ratio wrappers keep media, screenshots, and canvas previews stable across modules.

div.span: Shared tokens and interactions for client actions.
Reusable components for authenticated customer journeys.
Composable surfaces for editors, previews, and content tooling.
Stable viewports for screenshots, video ratios, and cards.
Searchable navigation and action dispatch from one shared system.
Hover and popover surfaces for maintainers, owners, and records.

CardHeader.CardDescription: Scrollable preview rails cover portal galleries, onboarding walkthroughs, and builder stage snapshots with keyboard-friendly controls and RTL-aware horizontal movement.

CardContent.p: Focus the carousel viewport to use arrow keys, or use the previous and next controls to move between full-width slides.

CardHeader.CardDescription: Desktop-style menu surfaces now support both config-driven menus and composed OWL item families, with the same side or align placement API as the lighter overlays and RTL-aware horizontal menubar movement.

ContextMenuTrigger.p: Legacy items arrays now support nested submenu entries as well.
Use mouse context-click or keyboard Shift+F10 to open the OWL-composed family.

CardHeader.CardDescription: Shared viewport navigation now supports both config-driven menus and a composed family of list, item, trigger, link, content, and indicator primitives, with RTL-aware horizontal trigger movement.

div.p: Pass item metadata when the menu is driven by route maps or registry-level configuration.
Use explicit triggers, links, and shared content panels when the menu needs richer custom markup.

a.p: Shared components, tokens, and services for backend actions, portal pages, and builders.

NavigationMenuLink.title: 'Installation'
NavigationMenuLink.description: 'Mount the bundle once and reuse the same primitives across addons.'

NavigationMenuLink.title: 'Theme tokens'
NavigationMenuLink.description: 'Light, dark, and semantic variables aligned across backend and portal surfaces.'

NavigationMenuLink.title: 'Overlays'
NavigationMenuLink.description: 'Dialog, sheet, popover, hover card, and alert dialog.'

NavigationMenuLink.title: 'Selection'
NavigationMenuLink.description: 'Select, combobox, radio group, checkbox, switch, and slider.'

NavigationMenuLink.title: 'Navigation'
NavigationMenuLink.description: 'Breadcrumb, pagination, menubar, context menu, and navigation menu.'

CardContent.p: This mirrors the shadcn-style pattern of trigger items, a shared viewport panel, and a compact indicator aligned to the active entry.

CardHeader.CardDescription: Provider-driven sidebars cover admin navigation, builders, and portal workspaces with collapsed icon mode, inset content, and direction-aware start or end docking.

div.p: Shared navigation for apps, builders, and portals.
The sidebar shell leaves the main canvas fully composable for builders, previews, or data entry.
Drop in forms, tables, or builders without rewriting the navigation frame.
Collapsed icon mode keeps the shell compact while preserving tooltip titles and focus targets.

CardHeader.CardDescription: Single and range date selection cover release planning, scheduling, and portal workflows, with bounded navigation across date constraints, locale-aware week starts, and RTL-aware horizontal navigation.
Stable file paths for imports and a dedicated frontend bundle for portal pages.

pre.code:
<Form descriptions="fieldDescriptions" errors="fieldErrors" onSubmit="() => submitRecord()"> <FormField name="'email'" required="true"> <FormItem> <FormLabel>Email</FormLabel> <FormControl t-slot-scope="control"> <Input attrs="control.attrs" type="'email'" value="state.email" onInput="(value) => state.email = value"/> </FormControl> <FormDescription/> <FormMessage/> </FormItem> </FormField> </Form>
<InputGroup> <InputGroupAddon align="'inline-start'"> <InputGroupText>https://</InputGroupText> </InputGroupAddon> <InputGroupInput value="state.slug" onInput="(value) => state.slug = value"/> </InputGroup>
<DropdownMenu> <t t-set-slot="trigger"> <DropdownMenuTrigger variant="'outline'">Release controls</DropdownMenuTrigger> </t> <DropdownMenuContent> <DropdownMenuLabel text="'Review policy'"/> <DropdownMenuSeparator/> <DropdownMenuRadioGroup value="state.reviewPolicy" onValueChange="(value) => state.reviewPolicy = value"> <DropdownMenuRadioItem value="'manual'" text="'Manual review'"/> <DropdownMenuRadioItem value="'automatic'" text="'Automatic merge'"/> </DropdownMenuRadioGroup> <DropdownMenuSub> <t t-set-slot="trigger"> <DropdownMenuSubTrigger text="'Jump to surface'"/> </t> <DropdownMenuSubContent> <DropdownMenuItem text="'Portal preview'"/> </DropdownMenuSubContent> </DropdownMenuSub> </DropdownMenuContent> </DropdownMenu>
import { Accordion, AccordionContent, AccordionHeader, AccordionItem, AccordionTrigger, } from "@odx_owl/components/accordion/accordion" import { Alert, AlertDescription, AlertTitle } from "@odx_owl/components/alert/alert" import { AlertDialog, AlertDialogAction, AlertDialogCancel, AlertDialogContent, AlertDialogDescription, AlertDialogFooter, AlertDialogHeader, AlertDialogOverlay, AlertDialogTitle, AlertDialogTrigger } from "@odx_owl/components/alert_dialog/alert_dialog" import { AspectRatio } from "@odx_owl/components/aspect_ratio/aspect_ratio" import { Avatar, AvatarFallback, AvatarImage } from "@odx_owl/components/avatar/avatar" import { Checkbox } from "@odx_owl/components/checkbox/checkbox" import { Collapsible, CollapsibleContent, CollapsibleTrigger } from "@odx_owl/components/collapsible/collapsible" import { Button } from "@odx_owl/components/button/button" import { ButtonGroup, ButtonGroupSeparator, ButtonGroupText } from "@odx_owl/components/button_group/button_group" import { Breadcrumb, BreadcrumbItem, BreadcrumbLink, BreadcrumbList, BreadcrumbPage, BreadcrumbSeparator } from "@odx_owl/components/breadcrumb/breadcrumb" import { Calendar } from "@odx_owl/components/calendar/calendar" import { Carousel, CarouselContent, CarouselItem, CarouselNext, CarouselPrevious } from "@odx_owl/components/carousel/carousel" import { BarChart, ChartContainer, ChartLegend, ChartLegendContent, ChartTooltip, ChartTooltipContent, DonutChart, LineChart } from "@odx_owl/components/chart/chart" import { Combobox, ComboboxContent, ComboboxIcon, ComboboxTrigger, ComboboxValue } from "@odx_owl/components/combobox/combobox" import { Command, CommandDialog, CommandEmpty, CommandGroup, CommandInput, CommandItem, CommandItemDescription, CommandItemText, CommandList, CommandLoading, CommandSeparator, CommandShortcut } from "@odx_owl/components/command/command" import { ContextMenu, ContextMenuCheckboxItem, ContextMenuContent, ContextMenuGroup, ContextMenuItem, ContextMenuLabel, ContextMenuRadioGroup, ContextMenuRadioItem, ContextMenuSeparator, ContextMenuSub, ContextMenuSubContent, ContextMenuSubTrigger, ContextMenuTrigger } from "@odx_owl/components/context_menu/context_menu" import { DataTable } from "@odx_owl/components/data_table/data_table" import { DatePicker } from "@odx_owl/components/date_picker/date_picker" import { DateRangePicker } from "@odx_owl/components/date_range_picker/date_range_picker" import { Dialog, DialogClose, DialogContent, DialogDescription, DialogFooter, DialogHeader, DialogOverlay, DialogTitle, DialogTrigger } from "@odx_owl/components/dialog/dialog" import { DropdownMenu, DropdownMenuContent, DropdownMenuItem, DropdownMenuLabel, DropdownMenuRadioGroup, DropdownMenuRadioItem, DropdownMenuSeparator, DropdownMenuSub, DropdownMenuSubContent, DropdownMenuSubTrigger, DropdownMenuTrigger } from "@odx_owl/components/dropdown_menu/dropdown_menu" import { Drawer, DrawerClose, DrawerContent, DrawerDescription, DrawerFooter, DrawerHeader, DrawerOverlay, DrawerTitle, DrawerTrigger } from "@odx_owl/components/drawer/drawer" import { Empty, EmptyContent, EmptyDescription, EmptyHeader, EmptyMedia, EmptyTitle } from "@odx_owl/components/empty/empty" import { Field, FieldContent, FieldDescription, FieldError, FieldGroup, FieldLabel, FieldLegend, FieldSeparator, FieldSet, FieldTitle } from "@odx_owl/components/field/field" import { Form, FormControl, FormDescription, FormField, FormItem, FormLabel, FormMessage } from "@odx_owl/components/form/form" import { Input } from "@odx_owl/components/input/input" import { InputGroup, InputGroupAddon, InputGroupButton, InputGroupInput, InputGroupText, InputGroupTextarea } from "@odx_owl/components/input_group/input_group" import { InputOTP, InputOTPGroup, InputOTPSeparator, InputOTPSlot } from "@odx_owl/components/input_otp/input_otp" import { Item, ItemActions, ItemContent, ItemDescription, ItemFooter, ItemGroup, ItemHeader, ItemMedia, ItemSeparator, ItemTitle } from "@odx_owl/components/item/item" import { Kbd, KbdGroup } from "@odx_owl/components/kbd/kbd" import { Label } from "@odx_owl/components/label/label" import { Menubar, MenubarCheckboxItem, MenubarContent, MenubarGroup, MenubarItem, MenubarLabel, MenubarMenu, MenubarRadioGroup, MenubarRadioItem, MenubarSeparator, MenubarShortcut, MenubarSub, MenubarSubContent, MenubarSubTrigger, MenubarTrigger } from "@odx_owl/components/menubar/menubar" import { NavigationMenu, NavigationMenuContent, NavigationMenuIndicator, NavigationMenuItem, NavigationMenuLink, NavigationMenuList, NavigationMenuTrigger, NavigationMenuViewport } from "@odx_owl/components/navigation_menu/navigation_menu" import { NativeSelect } from "@odx_owl/components/native_select/native_select" import { Pagination, PaginationContent, PaginationItem, PaginationLink, PaginationNext, PaginationPrevious } from "@odx_owl/components/breadcrumb/breadcrumb" import { Popover, PopoverAnchor, PopoverClose, PopoverContent, PopoverTrigger } from "@odx_owl/components/popover/popover" import { Progress } from "@odx_owl/components/progress/progress" import { RadioGroup, RadioGroupItem } from "@odx_owl/components/radio_group/radio_group" import { ResizableHandle, ResizablePanel, ResizablePanelGroup } from "@odx_owl/components/resizable/resizable" import { Select, SelectContent, SelectGroup, SelectIcon, SelectItem, SelectItemDescription, SelectItemIndicator, SelectItemText, SelectLabel, SelectScrollDownButton, SelectScrollUpButton, SelectSeparator, SelectTrigger, SelectValue, SelectViewport } from "@odx_owl/components/select/select" import { Sheet, SheetClose, SheetContent, SheetDescription, SheetFooter, SheetHeader, SheetOverlay, SheetTitle, SheetTrigger } from "@odx_owl/components/sheet/sheet" import { Sidebar, SidebarContent, SidebarFooter, SidebarGroup, SidebarGroupAction, SidebarGroupContent, SidebarGroupLabel, SidebarHeader, SidebarInput, SidebarInset, SidebarMenu, SidebarMenuAction, SidebarMenuBadge, SidebarMenuButton, SidebarMenuItem, SidebarMenuSub, SidebarMenuSubButton, SidebarMenuSubItem, SidebarProvider, SidebarRail, SidebarSeparator, SidebarTrigger } from "@odx_owl/components/sidebar/sidebar" import { Table, TableBody, TableCell, TableHead, TableHeader, TableRow } from "@odx_owl/components/table/table" import { Toast, ToastAction, ToastClose, ToastDescription, Toaster, ToastTitle } from "@odx_owl/components/toast/toast" import { HoverCard, HoverCardContent, HoverCardTrigger } from "@odx_owl/components/hover_card/hover_card" import { Tooltip, TooltipContent, TooltipProvider, TooltipTrigger } from "@odx_owl/components/tooltip/tooltip" import { Toggle } from "@odx_owl/components/toggle/toggle" import { ToggleGroup, ToggleGroupItem } from "@odx_owl/components/toggle_group/toggle_group" import { ScrollArea } from "@odx_owl/components/scroll_area/scroll_area" import { Slider } from "@odx_owl/components/slider/slider" import { Spinner } from "@odx_owl/components/spinner/spinner" import { useService } from "@web/core/utils/hooks"
const toast = useService("odx_toast") toast.add({ title: "Saved", description: "Record updated." })

CardHeader.CardDescription: Keyboard focus, semantic roles, aria state, and visible rings are first-class.

CardContent.p: Dialogs trap focus, restore it to the previous active element on close, expose reusable trigger, overlay, content, title, and description primitives, and now let callers intercept escape or outside interactions before dismissal.
Alert dialogs now expose dedicated overlay and content primitives while preserving alertdialog semantics and predictable cancel or action targets.
Popover, tooltip, and hover-card roots now accept shadcn-style side and align placement props while preserving the same focus, hover, and escape-dismiss interaction model.

Tabs implement roving focus with arrow keys, Home, and End behavior, now respect RTL horizontal direction, keep a dedicated tab stop during manual activation, support Enter or Space activation on the focused trigger, support force-mounted inactive panels when a workflow needs preserved DOM state, and emit a real root dir attribute for local RTL overrides.
Form wrappers compose labels, descriptions, and validation messages with generated ids and shared aria-describedby/aria-invalid wiring.
Collapsible sections preserve disclosure button semantics through aria-expanded, aria-controls, and labelled content regions.
Field sets and legends provide semantic grouping for related controls while field rows keep disabled and invalid states visible.
Checkbox and switch controls keep aria-checked synchronized with the rendered state, including mixed checkbox states via aria-checked="mixed".

Input OTP slots preserve sequential focus movement, paste distribution, backspace handling, a single hidden aggregated form value, RTL-aware left or right slot navigation, and real local dir propagation through slot groups.

Radio groups now use a single roving tab stop, arrow-key selection with RTL-aware horizontal direction, first-enabled fallback focus when no value is chosen, optional edge wrapping, a form-friendly hidden input value, and a real root dir attribute for local RTL overrides.
Accordion items wire aria-controls, aria-labelledby, disabled-state semantics, and Arrow/Home/End keyboard navigation across enabled triggers, including RTL-aware horizontal arrow behavior.
Progress exposes native progressbar semantics with current and max values, and indeterminate mode omits aria-valuenow while allowing descriptive value text.
Charts provide focusable hotspots or slices, shared legends, and live tooltip content while keeping series colors tied to a single config object.

Data tables preserve searchable headers, checkbox row selection, sortable columns via aria-sort, keyboard-reachable row actions, and logical start or end column alignment through root direction hooks.

Select roots now support both config-driven options and composed trigger, icon, viewport, item text, and item indicator primitives, plus buffered typeahead, highlighted-item auto-scroll for longer option sets, shared side or align placement props, and local direction hooks for logical inline alignment.

Combobox roots now support both the convenience command-backed picker and composed trigger, value, icon, and content primitives while preserving searchable disclosure behavior, stable trigger-to-listbox aria-controls linkage for the built-in command surface, the same overlay placement API, and local direction hooks that flow through the embedded command surface.
Native selects preserve browser-native semantics for fallback flows while matching the same token and invalid-state system.

Command roots support both config-driven items and composed input, loading, list, group, separator, item text, and item description primitives while preserving searchable listbox semantics with active-item keyboard navigation and logical inline spacing under local direction overrides.

Breadcrumb and pagination wrappers preserve semantic navigation landmarks for structured paths and paged data, now with RTL-aware separators and previous or next edge controls, while table primitives use logical start or end alignment instead of physical left or right text alignment.

Toggle buttons expose aria-pressed, and toggle groups now use a roving tab stop, Arrow/Home/End focus movement with RTL-aware horizontal direction, optional edge wrapping, pressed-state-aware fallback focus for dense toolbars, and a real root dir attribute for local RTL overrides.

Slider thumbs expose native slider semantics, support keyboard adjustment across the configured min, max, and step, now allow multi-thumb ranges to enforce minimum spacing between values, reverse horizontal positioning plus arrow behavior in RTL layouts, and emit real dir attributes on the root, track, and thumb surfaces for local RTL overrides.
Scroll areas keep a focusable viewport while custom rails reflect the current native scroll position.

Carousels expose a focusable viewport, keyboard slide movement, and explicit previous and next controls for sequential preview content, now with RTL-aware horizontal arrow mapping and mirrored control icons.

Dropdown, menubar, and submenu surfaces now accept shadcn-style side and align placement props while preserving the existing dropdown-position fallback strings, and their trigger or content shells now emit real dir attributes so logical spacing works under local RTL overrides.

Menubars preserve grouped items, checkbox and radio state, submenu affordances, and desktop menu semantics, now with RTL-aware horizontal trigger movement, mirrored default submenu direction when no explicit submenu side is provided, and logical inline spacing for shortcuts and inset rows.

Context menus open from mouse context-click and keyboard invocation while supporting composed trigger wrappers, grouped items, checkbox and radio state, nested submenus, initial focus movement into the rendered menu, aligned submenu placement, RTL-aware submenu opening keys and fallback direction, and logical inline spacing for labels or shortcuts.

Navigation menus preserve shared viewport disclosure, indicator placement, escape dismissal, and now expose both config-driven and composed APIs with RTL-aware horizontal trigger movement plus real dir propagation through trigger, list, and content shells.
Sidebar providers expose collapsed icon mode, inset content shells, grouped menu semantics, accessible toggle controls, and logical start or end side resolution with RTL-aware nested menu spacing.

Calendars render keyboard-operable date grids with bounded month navigation, roving focus, locale-aware week starts when weekStartsOn is not explicitly set, RTL-aware horizontal arrow movement, hidden ISO form values, disabled-date constraints, and real dir propagation through month, grid, and nav surfaces.
Range calendars keep start, middle, and end states aligned across multi-month views while preserving the same keyboard model.
Date pickers pair the same calendar primitive with popover disclosure and a formatted trigger surface, and now emit real root and trigger dir attributes for local RTL islands.
Date range pickers keep the popover open until the end date is chosen, then emit normalized from/to payloads while preserving the same local-direction hooks.

Sheets share dialog focus trapping, labelled-dialog semantics, and the same preventable escape and outside-interaction hooks, now resolve logical start or end sides against the active direction, and pass a real dir attribute through the sheet content surface.

Drawers reuse the same focus trap and dismissal model, including the new preventable escape and outside hooks, while exposing dedicated overlay, content, handle, and close primitives for stacked mobile or portal review flows, plus logical start or end support for horizontal drawer docking and a real dir attribute on the drawer content surface.
Resizable handles expose focusable separator semantics with orientation, current position, and keyboard resizing between adjacent panels, including RTL-aware horizontal arrow handling.

Popover, tooltip, and hover card families expose dedicated trigger and content primitives while sitting on Odoo's overlay stack; popovers now also support separate anchor and close primitives, and tooltips open immediately on focus while supporting provider-level shared delay tuning.

Dropdown menus support dedicated trigger primitives, grouped labels, checkbox items, radio items, nested submenus, keyboard navigation through Odoo's dropdown stack, and mirrored default submenu direction in RTL layouts.
Toast notifications expose polite live-region semantics by default, switch destructive messages to alert treatment, pause timed dismissal during hover or keyboard focus, and support swipe dismissal.
Theme changes set color-scheme and preserve user choice in local storage.
